nodes.title: Model Links
widgets_values:
# Kling 3.0 Motion-Control

This workflow is built for testing **Kling 3.0 Motion-Control** inside ComfyUI using the Kie.ai API.

It is designed for the image-plus-driver-video workflow:
- one reference image provides the subject, styling, and scene anchor
- one motion-driver video provides the movement
- an optional prompt helps steer the final result

Use this workflow when you want to transfer motion from a video onto a character or subject from a still image.

**Help Support Development**

Use this affiliate link when accessing Kie.ai:
[https://kie.ai](https://kie.ai?ref=e7565cf24a7fad4586341a87eaf21e42)

Project repository:
[https://github.com/gateway/ComfyUI-Kie-API](https://github.com/gateway/ComfyUI-Kie-API)

---

## Pricing Note

Kling pricing can change over time, especially across `720p` and `1080p` modes.

Pricing: only 12 credits/s ($0.06) for 720 p or 20 credits/s ($0.10) for 1080 p 

Before running long batches or higher-resolution jobs, check current pricing here:
[https://kie.ai/pricing](https://kie.ai/pricing)

---

## Included Node in This Workflow

### Generation Node

- **KIE Kling 3.0 Motion-Control (I2V)**
  - Generates a video from one reference image and one motion-driver video.
  - Accepts an optional prompt.
  - Supports `character_orientation` control.
  - Supports `720p` and `1080p` output modes.

---

## Core Inputs

### Reference Image

The reference image defines the subject, styling, clothing, face, and overall visual identity.

Best results usually come from:
- a clear subject
- strong lighting
- a readable pose
- minimal blur

### Motion-Driver Video

The motion-driver video supplies the movement pattern for the final output.

Best results usually come from:
- one clear performer or subject
- stable framing
- clean motion without heavy cuts
- movement that matches the pose logic of the reference image

### Prompt

The prompt is optional in this node.

Use it when you want to reinforce:
- motion quality
- style consistency
- facial consistency
- camera behavior
- distortion prevention


---

## Character Orientation

`character_orientation` controls which source has more influence over how the subject is facing and aligned during motion transfer.

- `image`
  - Tries to preserve the orientation from the reference image.
  - Use this when the still image composition matters most.
  - Kie docs say this mode supports driver videos up to `10s`.

- `video`
  - Tries to follow the orientation seen in the driver video.
  - Use this when accurate transferred body motion matters most.
  - Kie docs say this mode supports driver videos up to `30s`.

Simple rule:
- use `image` for stronger identity/composition lock
- use `video` for stronger motion-following behavior

---

## Recommended Workflow Pattern

1. Load a strong reference image.
2. Load a clean driver video.
3. Start with `character_orientation = video`.
4. Start with `mode = 720p` for cheaper iteration.
5. Add a short prompt only if you need extra control.
6. Move to `1080p` only after the motion behavior looks correct.

---

## Tips for Better Results

- Match the subject framing between the image and the motion video as closely as possible.
- If the image is front-facing and the video is profile-heavy, expect more drift or awkward pose conversion.
- Avoid overly complex backgrounds during early testing.
- Start with short, clean driver clips before trying longer or more chaotic motion.
- If identity drift appears, try `character_orientation = image`.
- If motion feels stiff or under-transferred, try `character_orientation = video`.

---

## Notes on Usage & Debugging

- All requests are sent to Kie.ai:
  [https://kie.ai](https://kie.ai?ref=e7565cf24a7fad4586341a87eaf21e42)

- If a node fails or times out in ComfyUI, check account logs:
  [https://kie.ai/logs](https://kie.ai/logs)

- Logs are usually the fastest way to verify whether a generation completed successfully.

- The Kie docs for this endpoint have one known inconsistency:
  - `mode` is described in one place as `std/pro`
  - the options table and request example use `720p/1080p`
  - this node currently uses `720p/1080p`

---

## Issues & Feedback

To report issues or request improvements:
[https://github.com/gateway/ComfyUI-Kie-API/issues](https://github.com/gateway/ComfyUI-Kie-API/issues)

Please include:
- node name
- parameters used
- error message or log excerpt

---
